title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: U.S. researchers face new restrictions on publishing with foreign collaborators
items.description: NIH, NASA grantees are confused and concerned amid agencies’ piecemeal communication

items.title: Microbiologist wins Georgia primary for U.S. House seat
items.description: Jasmine Clark is set to become the first Black congresswoman with a science Ph.D.

items.title: Not alive, but not dead: disembodied human brains used for drug testing
items.description: By restoring some functions to intact brains from deceased donors, the startup Bexorg hopes to create a better drug development test bed for neurodegenerative diseases

items.title: New study strengthens idea that humans evolved from knuckle-walking ancestors
items.description: Analysis charts evolution of the joint that made our species so nimble

items.title: Vaccine experts debate options to combat outbreak of unusual Ebola strain
items.description: Small monkey experiments raise some hopes—and many questions

items.title: Can extra snoozing reverse the health hazards of a bad night’s sleep?
items.description: Catching more z’s the day after a shortened slumber appears to reduce risk of early death

items.title: A new genetically modified rice could improve children’s health. But will it be grown?
items.description: Philippines takes first step toward approving rice with increased iron and zinc

items.title: Scientists play catch-up to startling Ebola outbreak
items.description: Testing, sequencing, and clinical trial efforts spring to life as Bundibugyo virus spreads

items.title: ¿Este científico fue demasiado lejos en su intento por salvar la vida silvestre de Ecuador?
items.description: Los esfuerzos de Alejandro Arteaga por identificar y proteger reptiles y anfibios tropicales lo han envuelto en controversia

items.title: BREAKING: WHO declares major outbreak of rare Ebola virus species an international emergency
items.description: No vaccines or treatments exist for Ebola Bundibugyo, which has caused hundreds of suspected cases and 80 deaths in Congo and Uganda; WHO warns of “potentially much larger outbreak”
